concerns.text: Task T035 (Phase 6) attempts to modify `contracts/PaperManifest.json` AFTER tasks T006 (Phase 2) and T013-T019 (Phase 3) have already implemented logic consuming the `PaperManifest` schema. A schema change must precede any consumer implementation. This violates the 'Producer before Consumer' rule.

Task T036 updates `code/ingest.py` to extract new fields. However, `code/ingest.py` is already implemented in T005 and T015 (Phase 2/3) based on the original schema. Implementing new extraction logic after the consumer code is written creates a dependency inversion where the consumer runs before the producer (schema) is updated.

Task T037 modifies `code/metrics.py` to calculate new statistics. `code/metrics.py` is defined in T007 (Phase 2) and consumed in T013-T019 (Phase 3). Modifying the producer (metrics.py) after consumers (model_runner/main) are implemented breaks the data-flow order.

Task T039 updates the `ReproResult` schema. `ReproResult` is generated by T018 (Phase 3) and consumed by T031 (Phase 5). Updating the schema definition in Phase 6, after the generator (T018) and consumer (T031) are already scheduled, violates the producer-consumer ordering.

Task T040 updates `code/stats.py` to include new metadata. `code/stats.py` is implemented in T023-T027 (Phase 4). Modifying the producer in Phase 6 after the consumer logic (T031) and the producer itself (T023) are defined creates a circular or inverted dependency.

Task T041 updates `code/guidelines.py`. `code/guidelines.py` is implemented in T031-T034 (Phase 5). Modifying the producer in Phase 6 after the implementation is complete violates the 'Foundational tasks first' principle for schema-driven code.

Phase 6 is labeled 'Revision & Robustness' but contains tasks that fundamentally alter the data model (contracts) and core logic (ingest, metrics, stats) which are prerequisites for the user stories. These tasks should be moved to Phase 2 (Foundational) or Phase 1 (Data Model) to ensure the schema and logic are established BEFORE the user story implementations (Phase 3-5) consume them.
